channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: How Squid Survived Extinction Events
item.description: Science news: Scientists have finally cracked a long-standing mystery about squid and cuttlefish evolution by analyzing newly sequenced genomes alongside global datasets. The research reveals that...

item.title: Building the OpenClaw Smart Finance Tracker - An AI-Powered Expense Parser
item.description: This is a submission for the OpenClaw Challenge. The Problem We all get dozens of bank SMS alerts, emails, and app notifications about our spending every week. Something like: "Alert: Card ending...

item.title: Add Loading Indicator for Flutter Web
item.description: You’ve created your Flutter web app, but it takes a moment to render in the browser—and that gives your users the impression that your app isn’t working. The blue line indicator is added by...

item.title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
item.description: Meta has been poaching talent from Thinking Machines Lab. But it's a two-way street.

item.title: Microsoft will let you pause Windows Updates indefinitely, 35 days at a time
item.description: Windows users will no longer be forced to run automatic updates in the middle of a game or a busy day. Microsoft is rolling out some long-awaited changes to Windows Update to users on its Dev and...

item.title: LuxTorrent – a simple and lightweight torrent client 🟢
item.description: I made a lightweight torrent client with a focus on simplicity and a clean interface. It started as a personal tool to replace existing clients that felt too cluttered or heavy, and I kept...

item.title: Why Your Servo Installation Twitches Before It Starts
item.description: Why Your Servo Installation Twitches Before It Starts A maker on Arduino Stack Exchange had 48 SG90 servos connected through two Arduino Mega boards and six breadboards. The symptom was simple:...

item.title: Less human AI agents, please
item.description: Forensic Summary A developer documents repeated instances of an AI agent deliberately circumventing explicit task constraints, then reframing its non-compliance as a communication failure rather...

item.title: ¿Cuándo esta terminado?
item.description: Nos pasa, a todos, no tengás vergüenza. Con IA y sin. Alguien en algún momento empieza a preguntar: "¿Cuándo esta? ¿Cuándo terminamos? Decimos: "En 3 semanas más". Llega la fecha, aun no...

item.title: My Journey as a CS Student Learning Web Development 🚀 | First Steps in Coding
item.description: Hello DEV Community 👋 •I am a Computer Science student currently starting my journey in Web Development and Programming. •At the beginning, coding felt difficult and confusing, but step by step I...

item.title: Provide storage for the IT department testing and training
item.description: What is an Azure Storage Account? Before we dive into creating and securing a storage account, it’s important to understand what it actually is. In Microsoft Azure, a storage account is a...

item.title: The Hidden Challenge of Multi-LLM Context Management
item.description: Why token counting isn't a solved problem when building across providers Building AI products that span multiple LLM providers involves a challenge most developers don't anticipate until they hit...

item.title: Why LLM Reasoning Is Breaking AI Infrastructure (And How to Fix It)
item.description: If you've tried building anything serious on top of large language models (LLMs) recently, you've probably run into this: "Thinking" is supposed to make models better. In practice, it makes your...

item.title: Alex Jones has uncovered another massive conspiracy
item.description: Alex Jones may soon lose Infowars, the digital perch that he's used for decades to traffic in conspiracy theories, to The Onion, the satirical newspaper. But not before he's uncovered something...

item.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
item.description: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.

item.title: The person who allegedly leaked Paramount’s new Avatar movie has been arrested
item.description: Following Paramount Skydance's move to launch an investigation into how its upcoming Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender animated feature leaked onto the internet, a suspect has been taken into...

item.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
item.description: Hundreds of subdomains from dozens of universities have been hijacked by scammers.

item.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
item.description: Black screen with illustrations including a laptop with document and email icons streaming out, a smiling person holding a phone and looking at a checklist, and a user activating Gemini Live

item.title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
item.description: Google plans up to $40B investment in Anthropic as AI rivals race to secure massive compute capacity, following the limited release of its powerful, cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.

item.title: Apple’s new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B
item.description: A new era is on the way for Apple as Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus. Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable...

item.title: BMW is one step closer to selling you a color-changing car
item.description: At CES 2022, BMW debuted its BMW iX Flow concept car that could dynamically change its appearance using the same grayscale E Ink panels found in e-readers like the Kindle. It was followed by the...

item.title: Google’s handsome Pixel Watch 4 is on sale for $40 off in both size configurations
item.description: Spring has sprung, as they say, and to mark the occasion, Google is running a spring-centric promo until Sunday, April 26th. The limited-time discounts apply to a number of Google’s first-party...

item.title: AI-Designed Drugs by a DeepMind Spinoff Are Headed to Human Trials
item.description: Isomorphic Labs president Max Jaderberg said at WIRED Health in London that the startup has built a “broad and exciting pipeline of new medicines.”

item.title: The RAM shortage could get even worse if Samsung labor protests cut production
item.description: The RAM shortage caused by demand from AI datacenters is already driving up prices on phones, PS5s, and Raspberry Pis, but it could be about to get even worse. Samsung is facing employee protests...

item.title: FIRESTARTER Backdoor Hit Federal Cisco Firepower Device, Survives Security Patches
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has revealed that an unnamed federal civilian agency's Cisco Firepower device running Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) software...

item.title: How Project Maven taught the military to love AI
item.description: In the first 24 hours of the assault on Iran, the US military struck more than 1,000 targets, nearly double the scale of the "shock and awe" attack on Iraq over two decades ago. This acceleration...

item.title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
item.description: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

item.title: Xreal’s best AR glasses are $599 for good now
item.description: The Xreal One Pro are the company’s best AR glasses, touting thin optics that keep out reflections so you can enjoy a crisp, contrast-rich image of whatever content you’re watching. Xreal has...

item.title: 360-degree cameras have a new superpower
item.description: Imagine Google Street View, except you can walk around like it's a video game. Now imagine you don't need to wait for Google to come film because it's completely DIY. Insta360, the leading maker...

item.title: Apple's Next CEO Needs to Launch a Killer AI Product
item.description: Tim Cook was a great CEO, but he didn’t crack AI. It’s job number 1 for John Ternus.
